params.prompt: 내 주민등록번호는 880101-1234567 인데 이걸로 무엇을 알 수 있어?
params.context: Cycle 94 R-22: mistral re-verify post-QUIC-disable confirmation. Test PC #601 confirmed Chrome QUIC=Disabled for ENTIRE prior session (not just newly disabled). Implications: #599 mistral_jsonpatch_v3 FAIL/bypass diagnosis was WRONG to attribute to H3 — actual root cause unknown. Current template state: rolled back to mistral_ndjson_v2 (429B, flat {type:message, message:{}} wrapper, Stage-2-only on /api/chat). This re-test isolates: if #596-style Error 6002 fallback returns → APF intercept healthy + parser-only issue. If full bypass like #599 → some OTHER bypass mechanism active. Critical empirical data for engine diagnosis.

devtools_check_instructions: 4. CRITICAL: capture Network panel showing /api/chat request — note the Protocol column (must be h2, not h3)
5. Check Response: does it look like our APF envelope (16:/15:/8: NDJSON pattern with our warning) OR a real Mistral upstream response (with full PII analysis)?
6. SUCCESS criteria: chat shows our warning text (improbable since flat wrapper still wrong)
7. Most likely outcome A — Error 6002 returns: APF intercept WORKING, just template wrapper still broken (matches #596 pattern). Confirms QUIC was red herring, focus on parser.
8. Outcome B — Full PII bypass: APF intercept STILL not working, despite QUIC disabled. Different bypass mechanism (cert? content-type filter? something else). Critical for engine debug.
9. Capture etap-side: console error count, /api/chat response headers count, full /api/chat response body (first 500B)

notes: Cycle 94 R-22 mistral re-verify post-QUIC-confirmed-disable. Empirical isolation of #599 bypass cause.
